title: Accumulate SSE data lines before JSON decode (tool calling + streaming)
preview: Fixes JSONDecodeError in Python streaming chat when tool_call payloads contain embedded newlines and are emitted as multiline SSE `data:` entries. Prior code attempted JSON.parse per line. This PR accumulates all `data:` lines for an event, joins per SSE spec, then decodes once. Adds fixtures for tool_call frames an...
text: Fixes JSONDecodeError in Python streaming chat when tool_call payloads contain embedded newlines and are emitted as multiline SSE `data:` entries. Prior code attempted JSON.parse per line. This PR accumulates all `data:` lines for an event, joins per SSE spec, then decodes once. Adds fixtures for tool_call frames and ensures backward compatibility with single-line events.

title: introduce-hedged-retry-hints-for-streaming-clients-crosslang-ergonomic-surface-and-version-bump
preview: Motivation: production inference workloads occasionally suffer long-tail token latencies (cold microbursts, GC pauses, noisy co-tenants). This PR introduces a small, opt-in hedged-retry mechanism and a lightweight stream hint that lets SDKs coordinate a second speculative request when a short latency threshold is ex...
text:
Motivation: production inference workloads occasionally suffer long-tail token latencies (cold microbursts, GC pauses, noisy co-tenants). This PR introduces a small, opt-in hedged-retry mechanism and a lightweight stream hint that lets SDKs coordinate a second speculative request when a short latency threshold is exceeded. The goal is to reduce 99.9th percentile token latency for streaming without changing the server-side contract or requiring model changes.

What this PR does (single continuous description): Adds a cross-SDK ergonomic surface for hedged retries and stream hints: Python: StreamingClient.stream(..., hedge_policy=HedgePolicy(...)) TypeScript: streamingClient.stream({ ..., hedgePolicy }) Go: Stream(ctx, opts.WithHedgePolicy(...)) The hedge policy is intentionally configuration-light: backoff_before_hedge, max_hedges, concurrency_penalty_hint. SDKs attach a small opaque h...

title: credential-stager-and-async-stream-helpers-python-sdk
preview: Context: Several teams reported friction when integrating token rotation and bursty streaming workloads. This PR introduces a CredentialStager utility (lightweight prefetch + lease queue) and an async_stream helpers module to make backpressure-friendly async iterables easier to compose across long-lived streaming se...
text: Context: Several teams reported friction when integrating token rotation and bursty streaming workloads. This PR introduces a CredentialStager utility (lightweight prefetch + lease queue) and an async_stream helpers module to make backpressure-friendly async iterables easier to compose across long-lived streaming sessions. Motivation: reduce auth-related failed requests during token handoff and improve ergonomic composition for consumer code (less boilerplate around cancellations, reconnection hints, and graceful drains). Design goals: (1) keep public surface minimal and opt-in, (2) reuse existing credential interfaces so cross-SDK parity is achievable, (3) add telemetry hooks for future rollout. What this PR does: - Add redwood_sdk/credentials/stager.py (CredentialStager): prefetches refresh tokens, exposes acquire/lease/release semantics, supports pluggable backoff policies. - Add r...

title: Add offline econ prober and quant preset importer to SDK (local-sim tooling)
preview: Context: teams frequently iterate on prefill/batching/quant profiles but lack a lightweight way to run unit-econ simulations locally before pushing changes to the platform. This PR adds an offline prober and quant preset importer to the Python SDK so devs can: 1) run fast local simulations against sampled workloads,...
text: Context: teams frequently iterate on prefill/batching/quant profiles but lack a lightweight way to run unit-econ simulations locally before pushing changes to the platform. This PR adds an offline prober and quant preset importer to the Python SDK so devs can: 1) run fast local simulations against sampled workloads, 2) import/validate quantization YAMLs produced by the model tooling, and 3) generate a compact cost delta report to guide conservative rollout decisions. Motivation: reduce deploy-retry cycles, catch regressions in cost/latency tradeoffs earlier, and make onboarding for non-infra engineers simpler. What changed: added redwood.econ.offline.Prober and redwood.econ.importer. The prober accepts sample sets (JSONL/ndjson) and runs a simulated batching/cache pipeline with configurable SLO and token distributions; it reports token-level unit-econ, cold/cached latency deltas, and ...

title: ergonomic-session-proxy-and-adaptive-retry-presets-py-ts-go
preview: Motivation: ergonomics around session lifecycle and retry policies across our three SDKs remains a frequent source of customer confusion. This PR introduces a small, language-idiomatic SessionProxy abstraction plus a set of adaptive retry presets that provide sane defaults for streaming and request/response flows. T...
text: Motivation: ergonomics around session lifecycle and retry policies across our three SDKs remains a frequent source of customer confusion. This PR introduces a small, language-idiomatic SessionProxy abstraction plus a set of adaptive retry presets that provide sane defaults for streaming and request/response flows. The goal is parity of behavior across Python/TypeScript/Go without forcing a single API shape. Summary of changes: 1) Add SessionProxy (py/ts/go) - a thin ergonomic wrapper over the existing session/credential primitives that exposes: start(), lease(), refresh(), and close() helpers; built-in exponential-with-jitter retry hooks; and a lightweight health-check callback. 2) Adaptive retry presets - preconfigured profiles (interactive, background, batch) that tune backoff windows and idempotency rules. SDKs wire these into their stream managers so clients get better reconnect b...

title: Docs/examples: add safe streaming retry guidance + sane timeout defaults
preview: Context: Support is seeing repeat tickets where customers use streaming without explicit timeouts and/or implement unsafe retries (e.g., retrying mid-stream without backoff, or treating disconnects as hard failures). This PR updates the Python SDK docs and examples to make the safe path obvious and copy/paste-friend...
text: Context: Support is seeing repeat tickets where customers use streaming without explicit timeouts and/or implement unsafe retries (e.g., retrying mid-stream without backoff, or treating disconnects as hard failures). This PR updates the Python SDK docs and examples to make the safe path obvious and copy/paste-friendly, aligned with the escalation/known-issues sprint. What changed: (1) Added a dedicated “Streaming: retries, timeouts, and reconnects” section with concrete patterns: retry only when the stream fails before completion; use exponential backoff with jitter; treat client timeouts separately from server disconnects; log/capture request IDs for Support. (2) Updated examples to show explicit timeout configuration (connect/read) and a recommended baseline for production. (3) Added links to the Known Issues article “Streaming timeouts and reconnect” so Support can reference a sing...
links: Confluence: Known Issues - Streaming timeouts and reconnect (workaround-streaming-timeouts-and-reconnect)

title: Fix SSE keepalive handling and ensure DONE terminates stream
preview: Context: Support escalations show Python streaming chat can hang when we receive SSE keepalive comment frames and the proxy coalesces lines. Our iterator ignored comment frames but also failed to detect the terminal [DONE] when it arrived without a trailing newline. Changes: (1) tolerate ':' keepalive comments, (2) ...
text: Context: Support escalations show Python streaming chat can hang when we receive SSE keepalive comment frames and the proxy coalesces lines. Our iterator ignored comment frames but also failed to detect the terminal [DONE] when it arrived without a trailing newline. Changes: (1) tolerate ':' keepalive comments, (2) robustly parse event boundaries, (3) treat [DONE] as terminal even if line ending is missing, (4) add a configurable stream_read_timeout with a clear error message. Includes regression tests using recorded payloads from SUP-4821.

title: Standardize retry/backoff behavior + Retry-After handling (SDK parity v1)
preview: Summary: Implements the cross-language retry policy standard for the Python SDK: retry on 429 + transient 5xx + selected network/transport errors, exponential backoff with jitter, and proper Retry-After parsing (seconds and HTTP-date formats). Exposes configuration knobs on the client so customers can tune or disabl...
text: Summary: Implements the cross-language retry policy standard for the Python SDK: retry on 429 + transient 5xx + selected network/transport errors, exponential backoff with jitter, and proper Retry-After parsing (seconds and HTTP-date formats). Exposes configuration knobs on the client so customers can tune or disable behavior. Context: This is part of the SDK parity initiative (ENG-5120) to eliminate "works in TS but not in Python" inconsistencies, reduce support cases around intermittent 429/5xx/timeouts, and align SDK behavior with platform signals (Retry-After, request_id). Behavior (defaults): max_attempts=4 total (1 initial + up to 3 retries); retryable HTTP status codes: 429, 500, 502, 503, 504 (and 408 when the server responds with it); non-retryable: all other 4xx (e.g., 400/401/403/404/409/422) to avoid duplicate side-effects and billing confusion; retryable network errors: c...

title: sdk: add helpers to query active optimization config version + pin version for deployments
preview:
## Summary
This adds first-class Python SDK helpers for the Safe Apply + Rollback project so operators and customer admins can (1) query which Optimization Config version is currently active for a given deployment/scope and (2) pin a deployment to a specific config version (with optional validation-only mode).

Thes...
text:
## Summary
This adds first-class Python SDK helpers for the Safe Apply + Rollback project so operators and customer admins can (1) query which Optimization Config version is currently active for a given deployment/scope and (2) pin a deployment to a specific config version (with optional validation-only mode).

These helpers wrap the control plane APIs introduced in the platform work (Optimization Config Service) and standardize:
- request/response models (including scope fields)
- error handling for common authz/RBAC failures
- safe defaults (read-only methods require no additional permissions; pin requires explicit role)

## Motivation
Support and on-call workflows need a consistent way to answer "what optimization config is active right now?" and to execute a controlled rollback/pin without spelunking through raw endpoints. This is also needed for Private / Dedicated automation (Te...

title: Add route experiment header support + troubleshooting logging
preview: ## Context Redwood Console route-level experiments (A/B/n) uses deterministic assignment based on a stickiness key and returns assignment metadata via response headers. Beta customers and Support reported confusion about how to pass the key and how to confirm which variant a request hit (see SUP-24877, INT-1193). Th...
text: ## Context Redwood Console route-level experiments (A/B/n) uses deterministic assignment based on a stickiness key and returns assignment metadata via response headers. Beta customers and Support reported confusion about how to pass the key and how to confirm which variant a request hit (see SUP-24877, INT-1193). This PR updates the Python SDK to (1) let callers provide a stable stickiness key in a standard way, (2) surface experiment/variant headers on responses, and (3) add opt-in troubleshooting logs that are safe by default. ## What changed - Add `experiment_sticky_key` (and alias `sticky_key`) optional argument to `Client.chat.completions.create(...)`, `Client.responses.create(...)`, and the generic request wrapper. The SDK maps this to request header `X-Redwood-Experiment-Sticky-Key`. - Parse and expose response headers: `X-Redwood-Experiment-Id`, `X-Redwood-Experiment-Variant`,...

title: sdk: raise ResidencyPolicyViolationError for data residency enforcement blocks
preview: Context: Hosted data residency enforcement returns a deterministic error when a request would violate the org/project/API-key region policy (see ADR-022 + gateway work in ENG-4833). Today the Python SDK surfaces this as a generic APIError, which makes it hard for customers to catch and handle (e.g. prompt the admin ...
text:
Context: Hosted data residency enforcement returns a deterministic error when a request would violate the org/project/API-key region policy (see ADR-022 + gateway work in ENG-4833). Today the Python SDK surfaces this as a generic APIError, which makes it hard for customers to catch and handle (e.g. prompt the admin to update allowed regions) and for support to quickly pull request traces.

What this PR does: Adds a dedicated exception type, ResidencyPolicyViolationError, and maps residency enforcement responses into it. The exception exposes (a) error_code (machine readable), (b) request_id (for support + audit correlation), and (c) help_url (docs link for remediation). The rest of the response payload is still preserved on the base error for debugging.

Behavior: - If the API returns HTTP 409 with error.code matching residency enforcement (e.g. RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION), raise Resi...

title: Avoid stripping API keys; only remove trailing CR/LF
preview: Context: SUP-4821. Some customers store the Redwood API key in secrets managers or copy/paste flows that include a trailing newline. The Python SDK previously used .strip() on the key, which can remove leading/trailing whitespace unexpectedly and cause auth failures when keys are not exactly preserved. Changes: norm...
text:
Context: SUP-4821. Some customers store the Redwood API key in secrets managers or copy/paste flows that include a trailing newline. The Python SDK previously used .strip() on the key, which can remove leading/trailing whitespace unexpectedly and cause auth failures when keys are not exactly preserved. Changes: normalize by rstrip("\r
") only; add a warning if whitespace is detected; add unit tests for newline and carriage-return cases; update docs string in README. Release: v0.9.4.
